refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall ensure operational availability meets or exceeds the customer-defined target by minimizing downtime and ensuring rapid recovery from faults.
refined_fr.rationale: Availability KPI is decomposed to focus on minimizing downtime and ensuring rapid fault recovery, which are key contributors to operational availability.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure actual uptime and downtime over the evaluation period and compare against the customer-defined availability target.
sub_frs: Enable rapid fault detection and recovery

candidate_dps: Redundant system components to reduce single points of failure

potential_sources: Dependency on telemetry data from Vehicle and Teleoperation modules
Interaction with Safety Services for fault alerts
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP logically isolates fault monitoring and diagnostics, minimizing coupling by using well-defined telemetry and alert interfaces.

assumptions_risks: Telemetry data is reliable and timely
Recovery procedures can be triggered automatically
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider integrating automated recovery DP in next decomposition level to reduce manual intervention risk.

im_boundary.name: Safety Services
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for monitoring system health, fault detection, and issuing safety alerts to maintain operational availability.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives real-time operational data from Vehicle and Teleoperation modules.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Sends alerts to Safety Services and other modules upon fault detection.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Supports communication between Safety Services and other NGHE solution modules.

module_containment_scores.notes: Broad scope but less focused on fault monitoring specifics.
Contains telemetry sources but not responsible for fault monitoring.
Provides telemetry and tasking but not fault monitoring.
Highest containment score for fault monitoring and alerting functions.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Safety Services module is best suited to encapsulate fault monitoring and diagnostics to maintain availability with minimal diffusion.
containment_risks: Potential overload of Safety Services if fault recovery is also included
Dependency on timely telemetry data from other modules
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Evaluate splitting fault recovery into a separate module if diffusion risk increases.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP (real-time fault monitoring and diagnostics) directly addresses the refined FR sub-requirements and is logically decoupled.

diffusion_risks: Telemetry interface dependency on Vehicle and Teleoperation modules
Potential overload of Safety Services if scope expands
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Safety Services module shows strong containment for the DP with minimal diffusion, supporting clear ownership.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed by monitoring Safety Services logs and alerts against telemetry data and availability metrics.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of fault monitoring DP to Safety Services IM with well-defined interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Consider next level decomposition to separate fault recovery from monitoring to reduce diffusion risk.
Validate telemetry data reliability assumptions with Vehicle and Teleoperation teams.

decision.justification: Current decomposition level sufficiently refines the availability FR with a clear DP and IM assignment that passes all gates with manageable risks.
next_level_sub_frs: Enable rapid fault detection and recovery

revise.why: Future refinement should include automated fault recovery DP to complement monitoring and reduce manual intervention.

assumptions: Telemetry data is reliable and timely
open_risks: Potential overload of Safety Services module
evidence_needed: Telemetry data latency and accuracy metrics
Safety Services fault detection performance reports
